title: Today's AI Brief: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
summary: A look at Endava's AI-powered software delivery and OpenAI's Rosalind Biodefense initiative.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I'm looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, Endava is making waves by redesigning software delivery around AI agents. They're using tools like ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to accelerate software delivery, automate workflows, and build an AI-native culture across their enterprise.

According to a report on OpenAI's website, Endava is leveraging AI agents to transform their software delivery process. This is interesting because it shows how AI can be used to streamline and improve the efficiency of software development. It's like having a super-smart assistant that can help you get things done faster and better.

Also on the board is OpenAI's launch of Rosalind Biodefense. This initiative aims to expand trusted access to GPT-Rosalind for vetted developers and U.S. government partners. The goal is to advance biodefense, public health, and pandemic preparedness through frontier AI. My read is that this could have a significant impact on our ability to respond to future health crises.

What stands out to me is the way these developments are pushing the boundaries of what's possible with AI. From software delivery to biodefense, AI is being used to drive innovation and improve outcomes. And, as these technologies continue to evolve, it's likely we'll see even more exciting applications in the future.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
